introSection: Thunderbolt, previously known as Pippin, is a wooden roller coaster located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was originally built by John A. Miller in 1924 and was named Pippin.

In 1958, Pippin received three trains manufactured by the National Amusement Device Company, known as Century Flyers. A new front was designed for the station the same year.

In 1960, an air brake system was added by Rutledge Equipment.

In 1968, the ride was renamed Thunderbolt, coinciding with an expansion of the track headed up by Andy Vettel. The four drops down into a ravine were incorporated in the Andy Vettel-designed Thunderbolt coaster.

Thunderbolt was rated the #1 roller coaster by the New York Times in 1974, and it still is the most popular ride at Kennywood.

Part of the ride's structure was updated for the 1984 addition of Raging Rapids, which passes under it.

For the 2014 season, Thunderbolt received new front panels on its trains.

As of 2025, the Thunderbolt is one of only two coasters to still run the full sized Century Flyer trains. The other being Big Dipper at Camden Park.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Unpainted track with blue catwalks, red railings and white supports.

fullText:
Thunderbolt, previously known as Pippin, is a wooden roller coaster located at Kennywood in West Mifflin, Pennsylvania, USA.

The ride was originally built by John A. Miller in 1924 and was named Pippin.

In 1958, Pippin received three trains manufactured by the National Amusement Device Company, known as Century Flyers. A new front was designed for the station the same year.

In 1960, an air brake system was added by Rutledge Equipment.

In 1968, the ride was renamed Thunderbolt, coinciding with an expansion of the track headed up by Andy Vettel. The four drops down into a ravine were incorporated in the Andy Vettel-designed Thunderbolt coaster.

Thunderbolt was rated the #1 roller coaster by the New York Times in 1974, and it still is the most popular ride at Kennywood.

Part of the ride's structure was updated for the 1984 addition of Raging Rapids, which passes under it.

For the 2014 season, Thunderbolt received new front panels on its trains.

As of 2025, the Thunderbolt is one of only two coasters to still run the full sized Century Flyer trains. The other being Big Dipper at Camden Park.



Unpainted track with blue catwalks, red railings and white supports.
